title: Weekend React Habit Tracker Curriculum

selected_videos.title: Learn React With This ONE Project
selected_videos.curriculum_role: Project-based React foundation and primary implementation spine
added_topics: React and JSX fundamentals learned through a project
Components, props, composition, and reusable component patterns
React state with useState and derived state
selected_videos.inclusion_reason: The title strongly indicates learning React through one project, matching the learner's preference for implementation over theory. The assessment also identifies likely coverage of React/JSX fundamentals, components, props, composition, and useState-based state. The specific project and habit-tracker features should be verified before relying on it for CRUD or persistence.

selected_videos.title: React Vite Testing Tutorial For Beginners - Vitest Testing Crash Course
selected_videos.curriculum_role: Testing supplement for the completed React project
added_topics: Component and behavior testing with Vitest and React Testing Library, subject to verification
selected_videos.inclusion_reason: The title explicitly focuses on React, Vite, and Vitest testing, making it the strongest available candidate for the required testing capability. It should be used after the application works, and its React Testing Library and behavior-testing depth should be verified because the evidence is metadata-only.

selected_videos.title: How to deploy react app to production
added_topics: Deploying the finished React app to production, with the Vite and hosting workflow to be verified
selected_videos.inclusion_reason: The title indicates a focused lesson on deploying a React app to production and therefore provides a practical final step after implementation. The exact host, Vite workflow, and production-build details are not confirmed by the metadata and should be checked.

rejected_videos.title: Every React Concept Explained in 12 Minutes
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: The title suggests a broad 12-minute React overview, but the learner explicitly wants to avoid surface-level introductions. It does not provide evidence of project implementation or the required app capabilities.

rejected_videos.title: React Tutorial for Beginners
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: This is a substantial React beginner tutorial, but candidate 3 is a stronger project-based foundation aligned with the learner's stated preference. The title does not establish coverage of Vite, hooks with localStorage, habit-tracker behavior, testing, or deployment.

rejected_videos.title: React Hooks in ONE Shot 2025 [ EASIEST Explanation ] | React JS Tutorial
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: The title focuses on explaining React hooks rather than building a project, which conflicts with the learner's project-based preference. It may also spend time on hooks beyond the weekend app's needs, while useEffect/localStorage coverage is not confirmed.

rejected_videos.title: ALL React Hooks Explained in 12 Minutes
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: The title indicates an all-hooks overview compressed into 12 minutes, conflicting with the learner's rejection of superficial introductions. It does not establish hands-on useEffect, localStorage, or habit-tracker implementation.

rejected_videos.title: React Testing for Beginners: Start Here!
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: Although it may cover React testing, its roughly 77-minute duration is substantial for a supplementary topic, and the title does not confirm Vitest or React Testing Library. Candidate 6 is a more targeted alternative because Vitest and Vite are explicit in its title.

rejected_videos.title: Vitest Simplified
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: The under-five-minute title suggests a short orientation, which conflicts with the learner's preference to avoid surface-level material. It also does not establish React Testing Library or component and behavior testing.

rejected_videos.title: Testing In React Tutorial - Jest and React Testing Library
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: The title explicitly mentions React Testing Library but specifies Jest rather than the requested Vitest. Candidate 6 is a closer tool match because Vitest is explicit in its title.

rejected_videos.title: 🔥Day 41 - React 90 days Plan | React Unit Testing using Jest & Vitest | Complete Enterprise Testing🚀
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: The title frames the material as complete enterprise testing, which is disproportionate to the requested weekend-sized localStorage app and may introduce excluded production complexity. Candidate 6 is more appropriately scoped.

rejected_videos.title: Deploy React App | Deploy React to Netlify | React Deployment Tutorial
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: This is a brief Netlify deployment option, but candidate 11 already supplies a focused React production-deployment step with more substantial duration. The short candidate may be procedural or superficial, and Vite production-build coverage is not confirmed.

rejected_videos.title: Deploying a Static Website with Vite + TypeScript
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: The title specifies TypeScript and a static website, while the learner's weekend scope does not include advanced TypeScript and the target is a plain React app. It may address Vite deployment, but it is not clearly a better fit than the selected React deployment resource.

rejected_videos.title: How to Deploy MERN Stack Project on Vercel | Host Full Stack Frontend and Backend Free 🔥
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: The title focuses on deploying a full-stack MERN project, including backend hosting, while backend databases and APIs are explicitly excluded. It adds complexity unrelated to the localStorage-only app.

rejected_videos.title: GOOGLE AI STUDIO BLANK SCREEN PROBLEM ON VERCEL DEPLOYMENT
rejected_videos.rejection_reason: The title concerns a Google AI Studio-specific blank-screen problem on Vercel, with no clear evidence that it applies to a standard Vite React habit tracker. It does not address general project construction or the requested capabilities.

covered_topics: React and JSX fundamentals learned through a project, provisionally via candidate 3
Components, props, composition, and reusable component patterns, provisionally via candidate 3
React state with useState and derived state, provisionally via candidate 3
React component and behavior testing with Vitest, provisionally via candidate 6
Production deployment of a React app, provisionally via candidate 11
uncovered_topics: Vite setup, React development server, project structure, and production build are not directly verified
useEffect and synchronizing React state with browser localStorage
Controlled forms for adding and editing habits
Rendering lists, stable keys, conditional rendering, and empty states
Complete habit-tracker CRUD behavior: create, complete/uncomplete, edit, delete, and filter
Local-storage initialization, serialization, and malformed or missing-data handling
Basic React debugging with browser DevTools and React Developer Tools
Confirmed React Testing Library coverage and testing of this app's forms, persistence, and behavior
A complete weekend-sized habit-tracker build-along combining all required capabilities
limitations: The candidate pool contains no clearly identified habit-tracker build-along; candidate 3 is the closest project spine, but its project and feature set are unspecified.
All selected-video assessments rely on metadata because transcript retrieval was blocked, so topic coverage is provisional and should be verified before following the sequence.
The pool lacks a strong, explicitly project-based resource for controlled forms, localStorage synchronization and error handling, list rendering, filtering, and habit CRUD.
Testing is represented by a Vitest-focused candidate, but React Testing Library and behavior coverage are not confirmed by its title.
Debugging with browser DevTools and React Developer Tools is not represented by a clearly suitable candidate.
The learner will need to reserve substantial time for implementation and debugging because the selected resources do not collectively verify the complete application workflow.
